generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System temperature sensor modeled in BIM environment for real-time environmental monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System humidity sensor modeled in BIM environment for real-time environmental monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System occupancy sensor modeled in BIM environment for space utilization tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACControllerSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System HVAC system controller sensor for operational status monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACControllerDataTransmitted
]

instance LightingControllerSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System lighting controller sensor for energy management"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingControllerDataTransmitted
]

instance SecuritySystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System security system sensor for access and safety monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecuritySystemDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance HVACControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACControllerSensor
]

instance LightingControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingControllerSensor
]

instance SecuritySystemDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecuritySystemSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational static model integrating geometric, spatial, and semantic data of physical building assets"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
]

instance BIMEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic foundation combining geometric BIM models with device modeling for sensors, actuators, and controllers"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized operational data from physical building systems"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted,
                  HVACControllerDataTransmitted, LightingControllerDataTransmitted, SecuritySystemDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo IoTDataIngestionEnabler, DigitalTwinManagementEnabler
]

instance StructuredBIMRepresentation : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Automatically generated structured representations from BIM models via Model-Driven Engineering principles"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMToDTTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering pipeline transforming static BIM models into deployable Digital Twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidationService
]

instance IoTDataIngestionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono-based framework for real-time IoT data ingestion and edge computing processing"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance DigitalTwinManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto implementation for device representation, state synchronization, and twin lifecycle management"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalTwinLifecycleService, BidirectionalSynchronizationService
]

instance VisualizationPlatformEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine integration for rich, interactive 3D visualizations of building Digital Twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveMonitoringService
]

instance CollaborativePlatformEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Multi-disciplinary stakeholder collaboration tools supporting AECO workflows"
    DTDFVocab:enables CollaborativeDecisionMakingService
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System HVAC actuator with remote control interface enabled through Digital Twin framework"
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System lighting actuator with remote control interface enabled through Digital Twin framework"
]

instance SecurityActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System security actuator with remote control interface enabled through Digital Twin framework"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates and processes runtime data to provide up-to-date insights into building performance"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight
]

instance SimulationAndAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes dynamic simulations of building systems to evaluate design alternatives and operational scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationResultInsight
]

instance LifeCycleOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables data-driven optimization across entire building lifecycle from design to operations"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers predictive insights for equipment failure forecasting and energy consumption trends"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
]

instance DigitalTwinLifecycleService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Manages Digital Twin lifecycle including version control and runtime configuration"
    DTDFVocab:provides TwinLifecycleStatusInsight
]

instance BidirectionalSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures continuous synchronization between physical building systems and Digital Twin representations"
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationStatusInsight
]

instance ImmersiveMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides rich, interactive 3D visualization of building operations through Unreal Engine"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance CollaborativeDecisionMakingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Facilitates evidence-based decision making through shared Digital Twin environments"
    DTDFVocab:provides DecisionSupportInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates transformed models to ensure consistency between physical and digital representations"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

    base:desc "Real-time operational metrics and performance indicators for building systems"
]

instance SimulationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation outputs evaluating structural, HVAC, and energy performance scenarios"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for lifecycle optimization across design and operations"
]

    base:desc "Forecasted equipment failure probabilities and maintenance schedules"
]

    base:desc "Current state and version information of Digital Twin representations"
]

    base:desc "Real-time status of data synchronization between physical and virtual systems"
]

    base:desc "Interactive 3D representations of building operations and system states"
]

]

    base:desc "Remote adjustment of HVAC system parameters through Digital Twin interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

]

// C1: Environment
instance AECOBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations domain environment"
    base:contains BIMGeometricModel, BIMEnrichedModel, RealTimeOperationalData,
                 StructuredBIMRepresentation, TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor,
                 OccupancySensor, HVACControllerSensor, LightingControllerSensor,
                 SecuritySystemSensor, HVACActuator, LightingActuator, SecurityActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **bidirectional control flows** from the digital twin to physical infrastructure via the DT Framework, which **operates the physical building systems** by feeding **control signals back to Building Control Systems (BCS)**. Facility Managers interact with the DT to issue commands (e.g., adjustments to HVAC, lighting, or security systems), which are transmitted through the framework’s **runtime configuration management** mechanism. While the document does not specify exact command types or message formats, the system ensures **validated command execution** by leveraging the framework’s modular, service-oriented architecture. Feedback loops are implied through the **bidirectional data exchange**, where physical system responses (e.g., sensor confirmations, operational status updates) are relayed back to the DT for real-time monitoring. Conflict resolution and command confirmation are managed within the framework’s **centralized hub**, though explicit security or authentication mechanisms are not described in the provided text."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, min, or event-driven triggers) are not specified, the framework prioritizes **low-latency operational responsiveness** to support dynamic monitoring and control by *Facility Managers*. The system is designed to accommodate **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments to temporal granularity or synchronization policies without full redeployment, though no granularity thresholds (e.g., sub-second, hourly) are defined. The architecture implicitly supports **multi-scale temporal operations**, from real-time sensor data to long-term lifecycle management, by integrating with enriched BIM models (static) and operational DTs (dynamic)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **service-oriented architecture (SOA) with a centralized orchestration hub**, structured around **coordinated roles and system components** (as depicted in Figure 1). Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  - **Bidirectional Data Exchange**: The framework facilitates **real-time synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, with data flows managed via **standardized extension points** for interoperability.
  - **Modular Component Integration**: Services (e.g., analytics, control logic) are **dynamically integrated** and managed through **runtime configuration**, enabling **low-overhead updates** without full system redeployment. This modularity supports **evolutionary scaling** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems.
  - **Lifecycle Management**: The system orchestrates transitions from **enriched BIM models** (static representations) to **operational Digital Twins** (dynamic, real-time), with clear delineation of responsibilities:
    - *Building Control Engineers* handle **device modeling and deployment**.
    - *Facility Managers* interact with **real-time monitoring/control interfaces**.
    - *DT Engineers* extend capabilities via **custom service integration**.
  - **Resource Management**: The architecture prioritizes **scalability** (horizontal and vertical) and **maintainability**, with non-functional requirements addressing **low-overhead updates** and **accessibility** for users of varying technical expertise. **Interoperability** is ensured through **heterogeneous system integration**, though specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) are not detailed."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, creating a **semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components.
  2. **Model Transformation and Artifact Generation**: Using **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations.
  3. **DT Deployment and Synchronization**: The framework supports **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital twins, ensuring continuous synchronization. This phase enables operational monitoring and control by Facility Managers.
  4. **Runtime Extension and Evolution**: A **modular architecture** with standardized extension points allows DT Engineers to integrate **custom services and analytics**, enabling the DT to evolve with changing operational requirements. This phase emphasizes **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control interfaces.
  Governance is implicit through **stakeholder-specific roles** (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and **modularity**, ensuring alignment with operational and technical requirements throughout the lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **model fidelity** by bridging BIM’s static geometric representations with dynamic behavioral and analytical models through **device modeling** (sensors, actuators, controllers). **Validation and verification** are implicit in the **automated generation pipeline**, where BIM-enriched models are transformed into deployable DT artifacts with minimal manual intervention, reducing human error. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between BIM’s static data and real-time operational changes, though no explicit quantification methods (e.g., probabilistic models) are described. **Error detection and correction** are supported by the framework’s **real-time data exchange**, enabling continuous synchronization between physical and digital systems. **Quality assurance** is ensured through the **modular architecture**, allowing *DT Engineers* to integrate custom services for validation, though specific protocols (e.g., model calibration, benchmarking) are not detailed."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **centralized network topology** with the **DT Framework acting as a hub**, facilitating **bidirectional communication** between physical assets, BIM models, and digital services. Technical connections rely on **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for protocol-mediated data exchange, ensuring interoperability across heterogeneous systems. The framework’s **modular architecture** supports standardized extension points, allowing for seamless integration of custom services and analytics. While the document does not specify exact bandwidth, latency, or redundancy requirements, the system prioritizes **scalability** (from individual components to entire infrastructure systems) and **maintainability** through low-overhead updates. Security protocols are not explicitly detailed, but the framework’s emphasis on **interoperability** and **accessibility** for varied technical expertise implies robust underlying mechanisms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **dt_hosting_deployment** infrastructure supports **multi-platform deployment models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** configurations, tailored to the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain’s operational needs. Computational and storage requirements are **scalable**, accommodating both individual components and entire infrastructure systems (as per NF_X). The framework’s **modular architecture** enables **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, thus optimizing resource utilization. **Deployment models** include **BIM-driven initialization** (by Building Control Engineers) and **Facility Manager interactions** for real-time monitoring, with **DT Engineers** extending capabilities via service integration. **Availability and reliability** are assured through the framework’s non-functional requirements, emphasizing low-overhead updates and adaptability to evolving operational demands. The system prioritizes **holistic building life cycle management**, bridging static BIM representations with dynamic operational data."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external BIM tools** (e.g., device modeling platforms used by Building Control Engineers) to enable **enriched model deployment**, though specific tools (e.g., Autodesk Revit, ArchiCAD) are not mentioned. **Real-time data exchange** is achieved via **Eclipse Hono and Ditto**, which serve as middleware for bidirectional communication between physical systems and the Digital Twin. Interoperability is ensured through **standardized extension points** and a **modular architecture**, allowing seamless integration with **heterogeneous systems** (e.g., IoT sensors, enterprise asset management tools). While explicit standards (e.g., IFC, COBie) are not cited, the framework’s design prioritizes **cross-system compatibility** to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational data. Dependencies on external systems are managed via the DT Framework’s **centralized hub**, which abstracts integration complexities while enabling **enterprise-wide Digital Twin constellations**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **industry-standard practices** and **interoperability principles**, with a focus on leveraging **BIM as the foundational standard** for Digital Twin (DT) deployment in the construction sector. **Key Standardization Aspects**:
  1. **BIM as a Standard**: The framework builds upon **BIM (Building Information Modeling)**, which is identified as the **de facto standard in the construction industry** for representing building data. BIM models serve as the **semantic backbone** for DT deployment, with extensions to support dynamic BCS (Building Control System) components.
  2. **Model-Driven Standardization**: The proposed **modeling methodology** standardizes BIM semantics to ensure consistency in device modeling (e.g., sensors, actuators, controllers) and control logic definitions. This methodology is designed to **reduce ambiguity** and enable seamless transformations into DT artifacts.
  3. **Interoperability Standards**: The framework supports **interoperability** through standardized extension points for integrating custom services and plugins. While specific standards (e.g., OPC UA, IFC) are not explicitly named, the modular architecture implies compliance with **industry-wide interoperability frameworks** to ensure compatibility with third-party tools or legacy systems.
  4. **Modular Architecture**: The DT’s **modular design** includes standardized interfaces for service integration, aligning with **software engineering best practices** for extensibility and maintainability. This modularity ensures that new services (e.g., analytics, IoT integrations) can be added without disrupting existing functionality, adhering to **modularity and loose coupling principles**.
  5. **Certification & Compliance Frameworks**: The framework’s reliance on **automated transformations** and **real-time synchronization** suggests adherence to **industry certification requirements** for DT systems, though specific compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 19650, ISO 23221) are not explicitly mentioned. The use of BIM and MDE principles implies alignment with **construction and facility management standards** for data exchange and lifecycle management."
]
